decisions.summary: Bug 1 (labels): lifecycle.transitionTo(id, 'in_planning') in issues.ts start-planning route was gated on issue.source === 'linear' — GitHub issues never got the in_planning transition, so stale merged/verifying-on-main labels from prior pipeline runs were never cleaned up on re-plan. Fix: remove the source guard and call it for all tracker types.

Bug 2 (pause): reconcileStaleMergeStatus() in deacon.ts calls postMergeLifecycle(issueId, ..., {skipDeploy: true}) as an unawaited Promise for issues where a prior PR is merged but mergeStatus !== 'merged' in review_status (e.g., reset by resetPipelineVerdictsForWorkStartSync). This races with an in-progress planning spawn, pausing planning-pan-XXXX concurrently with it starting. Fix: check for active planning/work agents before calling postMergeLifecycle — if an agent is running/starting, skip the re-fire (the new agent is in progress and pausing it would contaminate its state with the prior merge cycle).

The pause reason 'awaiting close-out (verify on main)' is generic text used for ANY issue post-merge. The original bug report interpreted it as coming from PAN-1866, but the investigation shows it comes from PAN-1982's own prior pipeline run via the deacon reconciler firing postMergeLifecycle('PAN-1982', ...) concurrently with planning spawn.

hazards.summary: The deacon's staleMergeReconciled Set suppresses re-firing once per server session. When there IS an active agent and we skip postMergeLifecycle, we still add to staleMergeReconciled, so the reconciler will never re-fire for that issue in this session.
hazards.mitigation: This is intentional. If a new agent is running, the pipeline will handle lifecycle transitions. setReviewStatusSync(mergeStatus: 'merged') is still called unconditionally so the state is accurate.

resumePoint.description: Implementation complete. Both fixes committed. Run pan done PAN-1994.

sessionHistory.note: Investigated root causes of both bugs. Bug 1: lifecycle.transitionTo gated on source==='linear' in issues.ts — removed guard to fix GitHub label cleanup on re-plan. Bug 2: reconcileStaleMergeStatus fires postMergeLifecycle concurrently with planning spawn — added active-agent guard to skip re-fire when planning/work agent is running. Typecheck and full test suite (700+32 tests) pass.

No deviations. Fixed two root causes of cross-issue label/pause contamination on re-plan: (1) Removed source==='linear' guard on lifecycle.transitionTo(id, 'in_planning') in the start-planning route — GitHub issues now get stale label cleanup (merged, verifying-on-main, etc.) when re-planning starts. (2) Added active-agent guard in reconcileStaleMergeStatus to skip postMergeLifecycle re-fire when a planning or work agent is already running/starting for the issue, preventing the deacon reconciler from pausing an in-progress planning session with the prior merge cycle's pause reason.
